title: put() copies secret bytes into non-Zeroizing ByteString
description: lib.rs:168: bytes.to_vec() creates a ByteString(Vec<u8>) which is never zeroized on drop. CLAUDE.md invariant: 'Never copy inner bytes into a non-Zeroizing buffer inside any backend.' Result: secret bytes remain in heap memory after put() returns, exploitable via memory dump or swap.
